entries.name: Multi-Network Dashboard
entries.description: Network tiles → drilldown column + circular gauge. QTD AA by network.

entries.name: Ranker Report (Executive)
entries.description: Top-performing programs/networks by demo × daypart. Executive bar + grid.

entries.name: Network Program Trends
entries.description: Program rating trajectory over time. Multi-series line.

entries.name: Network Genre Analysis
entries.description: Genre share + trend. Combination column (share) + line (trend).

entries.name: TV Series Genre
entries.description: Series performance by genre segment. Column comparison.

entries.name: Program Tracker (Genre)
entries.description: Genre/program hierarchy by audience volume. Nested treemap.

entries.name: Minute-by-Minute
entries.description: Within-telecast audience curve. Promo/ad/program overlay. Smooth spline.

entries.name: Universe Tracker
entries.description: Universe estimate over time + composition. Line + pie pair.

entries.name: Duplication Report
entries.description: Audience overlap between programs/networks. Venn + OOB grids.

entries.name: Content Licensing Heat
entries.description: Territory × genre licensing opportunity heatmap. $279B market lens.

entries.name: Box Office Intelligence
entries.description: Theatrical performance: budget vs gross, ROI scatter, studio ranking. Budget/gross correlation.

entries.name: Audience Flow
entries.description: Lapsed · New · Retained viewer migration with Super-PID overlay. Where did viewers come from and go. Sankey / alluvial flow.

entries.name: Smart Grid
entries.description: Daypart × Day dynamic heatmap across all research metrics. RTG benchmark + competitive overlay, drill-down to telecast.

entries.name: Audience Profile
entries.description: Telecast / Series / Network 360: demo profile, reach, retention, premiere-vs-repeat split. Per-title lifecycle snapshot.

entries.name: DMA / Local Market
entries.description: Local-market viewership by DMA. US choropleth + rank. Pending PID-DMA mapping for respondent-level local cuts.

entries.name: Placement & Coverage Forecast
entries.description: Predicted Rtg% vs network Rtg% across candidate networks. Box-whisker delta bands. Powers Scoop Suggestions placement recs.

entries.name: %GSHR/%SCH Affinity Index
entries.description: Network / program affinity for a target audience, indexed to a 100 baseline. Efficiency of viewing vs schedule supply.

entries.name: Affinity Program Ranker
entries.description: Dual-population ranker (all vs heavy-segment), heat-shaded, with %GSHR/%Sch index column. Decodes what core viewers watch.

entries.name: Reach × Time-Spent Quad
entries.description: Genre / subgenre efficiency: Avg Reach% vs Avg Time-Spent Viewing. Bubble size = Share of GRP. Quadrant positioning.

entries.name: Perceptual Map
entries.description: Competitive landscape: all-nets / genres Avg Audience against M/F skew, median age, and HoH race. Demo positioning.

entries.name: Viewer Segmentation Matrix
entries.description: Gold / Occasional / Surfer / Lite frequency × duration segmentation. Composition, reach, minutes-per-day, viewing-day share.

entries.name: Segment / Persona Profile
entries.description: Segment KPI donuts (%audience, %minutes, skew, median age, reach) plus competitive program & network context tables.

entries.name: Supply vs Demand
entries.description: Share of GRP vs Share of Duration, paired bars. Surfaces over/under-scheduled genres — viewing demand against schedule supply.

entries.name: Studio / Distributor Share
entries.description: Studio / distributor share of viewing & duration across network groups. Rights and content-licensing lens.

entries.name: Tabular Reports
entries.description: Telecast · New/Lapsed PID · Metadata · Movie & TV Tracker · Forecast Audit. Generic Top-N data grid.

entries.name: Cable Ecosystem Health
entries.description: Universe estimate (UE), PUT/TUT, ICD/SVOD share trends by demo over time. Multi-series line showing cable universe erosion vs streaming growth. Cord-cutter overlay.

entries.name: Cord-Cutter / Cord-Never Profile
entries.description: Demographic, territory, county-type, SVOD ownership and MVPD provider loss profile for cord-cutters vs retained cable. Audience vulnerability lens.

entries.name: Network × Daypart Rank Grid
entries.description: Hour × DoW heatmap of network rank and average impressions. Color-coded green (best) to red (worst). Best/worst timeslot identification at granular hour-slot level.

entries.name: Black Share Gainers / Losers
entries.description: QoQ and YoY network share delta ranked by HOH Black P18-49 gain/loss. Annotated with content-shift driver. Horizontal waterfall + ranked bar.

entries.name: Top Viewer Segment Analysis
entries.description: Heavy / Gold / Occasional / Lite viewer composition: weighted tuning minutes, % of total viewing, YoY change. Multi-network comparison across competitive set.

entries.name: Viewer Duplication Matrix (Program)
entries.description: Program-level audience overlap: % of Program A viewers who also watch Program B. Heat-shaded cross-tab grid. Scheduling adjacency and acquisition intelligence.

entries.name: Top Viewer Device & Provider Profile
entries.description: Top-viewer penetration for Smart TV, SVOD, OTT device type, and MVPD provider. Bar + index gauge vs universe average. Tracks audience technology migration risk.

entries.name: Content Avails Tracker
entries.description: Quarterly movie vs TV series avails volume over time. Stacked area showing inventory depth by type and genre. Linked to scheduling opportunity gap vs demand.

entries.name: Content Spend Analysis
entries.description: Content investment by Original vs Acquired, Movie vs Series, and scripted sub-types. Pie + treemap with average annual spend by category. Budget allocation intelligence.

entries.name: Asset Allocation Optimizer
entries.description: Optimal Movie vs TV Series hours by daypart × DoW. Binary optimization model (AdVant™) output — current vs recommended allocation grid with predicted share lift delta.

entries.name: What-If Scenario Simulator
entries.description: Interactive strategy builder: select scheduling option (strip / movies-first / mixed / daily-focus) → live predicted share vs baseline. Evaluates Options A/B/C/D side-by-side.

entries.name: ML Feature Importance
entries.description: CatBoost / LightGBM feature importance scores by individual feature and category (Temporal Cyclical, Content Share, Viewing Pattern, Seasonal). Grouped horizontal bar.

entries.name: Content Acquisition Recommendations
entries.description: Ranked acquisition target list: title, suggested time slot, confidence level, lapsed-PID affinity score, total weighted minutes watched. Grid with heat-coded confidence column.

entries.name: Network Ratings Trend — Total Day
entries.description: P18-49 AA% (LSD) across total day, by network. QoQ and YoY % change annotated. Coverage-area qualified. Multi-series trend with YoY delta line overlay.

entries.name: Audience Retention & Lapse Analysis
entries.description: Retained vs. Lapsed vs. New viewer split (millions + %) QoQ. 6-minute qualifier threshold. Donut showing cohort composition, waterfall showing net audience delta by quarter.

entries.name: Lapsed Audience Migration Map
entries.description: Where lapsed PIDs went: top 10 networks and top 10 programs by weighted minutes absorbed. Ranked horizontal bar with YoY column. Sankey variant for flow visualization.

entries.name: Audience Attribute & Profile Report
entries.description: HoH Income, HoH Race SoV, Gender Skew, Median Age, device profile (iOS/Android/Broadcast-Only). Share of Viewing (SoV) indexed vs. universe. Diverging bars + radar profile.

entries.name: Top Viewer Concentration Analysis
entries.description: Top-N viewer cohort as % of total network viewing (Weighted Minutes/Week). Multi-network side-by-side. YoY change in concentration. Stacked bar split: Top Viewers vs. All Others.

entries.name: Multi-Network Competitive Ratings Trend
entries.description: Network vs. network Black P18-49 ratings trend across 24+ months. YoY % change annotated per network. PUT universe trend as reference baseline. Grouped multi-series line.

entries.name: Panel Cohort Tracker
entries.description: Tracks a defined PID cohort (e.g. Top 250 viewers) across two time periods: survivor count, migration to other networks, churn rate. Sankey flow + funnel showing panel attrition.

entries.name: ML Feature Importance — Ratings Drivers
entries.description: GAM / XGBoost / Random Forest feature importance scores. Positive vs. negative impact split. Top-10 driver ranking per network. Diverging horizontal bar with importance magnitude axis.

entries.name: Ratings Prediction & Timeslot Optimization
entries.description: Forecast AA(000s) by daypart × day-of-week. Strongest and weakest timeslots for specific content types (movies, premieres, African American demos). Day-Part Heatmap + data grid.

entries.name: Program Acquisition Scorecard
entries.description: Content fit scoring table: series/movie name, suggested timeslot, confidence tier (High/Medium/Low), lapsed-PID total weighted minutes, affinity rationale. Heat-coded confidence column.

entries.name: TV Series Genre Analysis — Audience Segment
entries.description: Top genres ranked by GRPs across custom 12-month AMRLD audience segments (P2+, P18-49, P25-54). Top programs per genre per segment. Grouped column with segment-toggle filter.

entries.name: Cross-Network Viewing Index
entries.description: BET (or target network) audience over-index on external network programs. %GSHR vs. %Schedule for candidate content. Affinity scoring via weighted minutes. Bubble scatter + indexed bar.

entries.name: Reach & Frequency — Program Schedule
entries.description: Unduplicated HH/Person reach + frequency by program schedule. Progressive cume reach curve. Distinct from AD04 (advertiser campaign R&F) — this is content/programming schedule R&F.

entries.name: Source & Destination Analysis
entries.description: Where viewers came from before and where they went after a specific program minute. Flow-based audience path analysis. Distinct from P41 (quarterly cohort lapse) — this is minute-level viewing event tracking.

entries.name: Length of Tune / Length of View
entries.description: Average duration of tuning events per daypart (LOT) and average length of viewing per program episode (LOV). Measures program strength and appeal; assesses impact of format changes on audience hold.

entries.name: NTile / Quad Viewer Segmentation
entries.description: Divides viewers into N equal groups by viewing frequency × duration. Heavy/Medium/Light/Non-viewer quartile breakdown. Distinct from P21 (Gold/Occasional/Surfer/Lite) — frequency-based NTile method from nPower respondent level.

entries.name: DVR / Time-Shifted Viewing Analysis
entries.description: Live vs. Live+SD vs. Live+3 vs. Live+7 vs. Beyond7 viewing distribution by program. TSV lift %, DVR playback share, and C3 ACM commercial rating impact. Time-shifting behavior by demo and series type.

entries.name: Co-Viewing Report
entries.description: Pairs of viewers watching the same program on the same set simultaneously. Household composition and co-viewing index by demo. Used to understand family/shared viewing dynamics for content and advertising.

entries.name: Program Persistence & Inheritance
entries.description: Episode-to-episode audience carry-over (persistence/retention) and inherited audience from lead-in program. Measures programming stack efficiency, lead-in contribution, and loyal vs. transient viewer split.

entries.name: Audience Migration & Switching
entries.description: Where viewers switched to/from at a program boundary — time-slot winner identification and tuning migration map. Distinct from P41 (quarterly cohort lapse) — this is real-time minute-boundary switching from AdvantEdge.

entries.name: Rating Grid & Daily Recap
entries.description: Hour-by-hour network ratings grid for any given day or week across all networks simultaneously. Multi-network, single-day AA(000) program flow. Comprehensive daily competitive view from MediaSpectrum.

entries.name: Telecast Ranking
entries.description: All telecasts ranked by impressions/ratings for a selected period. Program-level ranking across networks by demo. Distinct from P02 (Ranker Report) which is network/daypart executive bar — this is individual telecast level.

entries.name: Daily Ranker
entries.description: Programs ranked by impressions on a day-by-day basis within any selected week. Granular daily competitive ranking distinct from weekly or season averages. Surfaces day-specific winners and outliers.

entries.name: Live & C3 Ranker
entries.description: Side-by-side Live vs. C3 commercial ratings ranking across programs. Currency-grade ranking for ad sales. Shows which programs gain/lose the most from time-shifting on a commercial-minutes basis.

entries.name: PAC Index & PAC Trend
entries.description: Program Average of Commercials (PAC) indexed to Program Average. Answers what % of program viewers actually watched the commercials. PAC Trend shows week-by-week variation. Key retention-to-commercial metric.

entries.name: Skip Report — Commercial Avoidance
entries.description: Viewers who watched a program in its entirety vs. avoided a segment (promo, PSA, commercial break). AVO rate by daypart, pod, and program. Surfaces ad avoidance risk by content type and time placement.

entries.name: NTV / NHTV National + Hispanic Grid
entries.description: Side-by-side National People Meter (NPM) and National Hispanic People Meter (NHPM) ratings for selected programs and networks. Dual-sample report surfaces Hispanic audience index vs. national average for content decisions.

entries.name: MarketBreaks Demographic Report
entries.description: Custom demographic cuts against program/network ratings: race (White/Black/Hispanic), HoH income, education, SVOD ownership, PC ownership, county size, territory. Sub-demographic indexing beyond standard age/sex cells via nPower Market Breaks.

entries.name: Up-To-Date Daypart YoY Comparison
entries.description: Daypart averages across multiple demo targets with automatic YoY comparison to comparable month, quarter, year, and broadcast season from the prior year. Network-level pacing view from MediaSpectrum Up-To-Date module.

entries.name: HMLN Report
entries.description: 3-NTile (Heavy/Medium/Light/Non-viewer) segmentation of prior 28-day network viewers applied to a premiere telecast. Shows premiere audience composition % by H/M/L/N segment. Frequency-based prior-viewing segmentation distinct from P21 duration-based segmentation.

entries.name: In-Episode Audience Retention & Churn
entries.description: Minute-by-minute audience entry (Groups A1/A2/B/C/D by join time) and exit tracking within a single telecast. Metrics: total/avg minutes watched, % broadcast consumed, % who stayed to last 3 minutes, new + lost viewers, net viewers. Lead-in inheritance vs. fresh tune-in split.

entries.name: Propensity Index Report
entries.description: Top 100 cable + broadcast programs ranked by affinity index for Heavy and Medium viewers of a target series. NTile 1/2 CVG AA% indexed to composite. Diff-in-rank (Heavy/Medium vs. composite) color-coded +/−. Network font-coded: Broadcast = blue, target network = red.

entries.name: Road to Success — Premiere Forecast
entries.description: Pre-premiere forecast model: HMLN segment composition × lead-in duplication % × scatter estimate → projected premiere audience by H/M/L/N. Combines NTile segmentation, R&F duplication, and scatter planning into a single premiere estimate output.

entries.name: Quarterly Cumulative Network Reach
entries.description: 13-week progressive cumulative reach by week for a network, broken out by Heavy/Medium/Light viewer segments and multi-demo (HH, P25-54, M25-54, P18-49, M18-49, M18-34, P2+). Shows cumulative audience build week-over-week across the full quarter.

entries.name: Quarter-Hour Grid / Daypart Report
entries.description: Per-network, per-quarter-hour program and AA(000) ratings for a selected night or daypart. Shows program flow across a broadcast night at 15-minute granularity with daypart summary totals. More granular than P59 (hour-level multi-network daily recap).

entries.name: Future Programming Guide
entries.description: Forward-looking schedule grid (no ratings) showing planned programming by network and timeslot across a competitive set. FYI/scheduling data only — used for pre-air competitive planning before telecasts air. Possible combination into site report noted.

entries.name: Program Search Report
entries.description: Individual telecast ranker for a specific program searched by keyword/title. Shows AA(000), % change vs. ½hr lead-in, and % change vs. prior 4-week average by episode. Tracks series episode-level trending across a date range and competitive context.

entries.name: Audience Journeys
entries.description: Respondent path/migration across programs and networks — where an audience came from and where it went, at respondent grain. Backing contract: aud.v_amrld_program_enriched over the AMRLD mirror shells (SHELL-PARITY-001 → POPULATE lineage; gated on mirror coverage window). licensing_class: internal (respondent-derived aggregates only).

entries.name: New / Lapsed PIDs
entries.description: Audience gain/loss at network + program grain: PIDs newly viewing vs lapsed vs retained period-over-period, respondent-level. Backing contract: aud.v_amrld_program_enriched (AMRLD mirror lane). licensing_class: internal.

entries.name: Forecast Audit
entries.description: Prediction-vs-actual trail for viewership forecasts: forecast value, landed actual, error distribution, drift over time. Backing contract: sasmaster_ring3.ds.predictions (actuals backfill; ring-3 database — never shared). licensing_class: internal.

entries.name: Duplication Network-Benchmark
entries.description: Pairwise audience overlap for a target network/program vs a benchmark set — duplication percentages at respondent grain. Backing contract: AMRLD mirror lane duplication engine (gap card: no serving contract yet — engine derives from aud.v_amrld_program_enriched). licensing_class: internal.

entries.name: Awards Tracker
entries.description: Award-cohort performance: nominated/winning titles and people vs viewership and social response. Backing contract: Wikidata people-award lane (P166 family, wikidata_prd 2026 monthly harvest) — WALLED until populated. Status: pending-source (visible, not silent). licensing_class: internal.

entries.name: Third-Party-Measurement Tracker
entries.description: External-measure comparison: Nielsen currency vs third-party measurement (VideoAmp/iSpot/Comscore-class) for the same inventory. Backing contract: none yet — contract card required before build. licensing_class: REVIEW REQUIRED (Shiv sign-off pending — third-party measure redistribution terms unruled).

entries.name: Campaign Reach & Conversion
entries.description: On-air promo reach % and conversion by channel, daypart, Nielsen demo. Rolling campaign window.

entries.name: Post-Campaign Analysis
entries.description: Multi-platform performance: GRPs, reach, frequency by network group (On-Net / Off-Net / DMA / MVPD).

entries.name: Conversion Efficiency Scatter
entries.description: Reach vs. Conversion by channel — surfaces highest-ROI promotional outlets per dollar of inventory.

entries.name: Segment Affinity Matrix
entries.description: Network × Program affinity grid for custom proxy audience (PRIZM/MRI-seeded). Tile intensity = affinity index.

entries.name: Multinet Plan vs. Actuals
entries.description: Planned vs. delivered GRPs and impressions across all networks. Campaign timeline with compliance view (Gantt).

entries.name: Psychographic Cluster Map
entries.description: Nielsen PRIZM Premier 68-segment audience profile. Lifestage Group × Social Group × affluence clustering.

entries.name: Social Buzz Velocity
entries.description: Tweet volume × engagement rate × share of voice. 30-day rolling window.

entries.name: Franchise Bubble Map
entries.description: Franchise vs competitor social SOV. Bubble size = audience reach. Force layout.

entries.name: Genre Arbitrage Report
entries.description: Audience appetite vs content supply gap. Scatter + shaded opportunity zone.

entries.name: Promo Campaign Effectiveness Report
entries.description: On-air promo 1+/3+ reach by viewer segment (Heavy/Medium/Light/All), avg frequency, reach impressions, and dual-direction conversion (saw promo → watched show; watched show → saw promo). Segmented by campaign flight window (full campaign, pre-premiere, premiere week).

entries.name: Cross-Channel & Off-Channel Promo Analysis
entries.description: Per-network promo unit count, reach impressions, and conversion rates for cross-channel (sister networks) and off-channel (competitive) placements. Unduplicated reach + cross-channel lift % vs. on-channel baseline. Network-level ROI ranking for promo placement strategy.

entries.name: Pre/Post Launch Marketing KPI Dashboard
entries.description: Dual-phase campaign dashboard. Pre-Launch: OTX awareness/intent/network linkage, R&F pacing vs. goals, promo AVR, ePoll content interest, social engagement/sentiment/profile, digital visits by device. Post-Launch: live/L3/L7 ratings, digital viewership, new viewer %, audience migration, campaign reach/conversion by channel (on-air, cross-channel, off-air, social).

entries.name: Daypart Tracker vs. Goals
entries.description: Network daypart AA(000) vs. stated ratings goals, broken out by Prime, Total Day, Sales Prime, and other custom dayparts. YoY % change at week, month, quarter, and full-year granularity. Pacing view used to assess whether network is on track vs. annual targets.

entries.name: Season Report Card
entries.description: Full-season executive report: episode-by-episode ratings table, multi-season YoY comparison across all demos, time-shifting table (LSD/L3/C3/L7 lift %), AA audience composition (Afrcn/Non-Afrcn), audience profile (HoH Race, Income, Education, Territory), lead-out destination analysis (SOV %), top affinity programs (Heavy/Medium viewer ranker — cable + broadcast), repeat performance by daypart vs. network norms.

entries.name: POD Intelligence (Position, Audience, Retention)
entries.description: Position bar ranking + retention curve (line). Viewership drop-off by content daypart. Nielsen + Gracenote-mapped.

entries.name: Network Zero Share Report
entries.description: Networks with zero spend by Category·ParentCo·Advertiser·Brand. Treemap + bar drill.

entries.name: Platform/Network Spend
entries.description: Spend hierarchy by group/cluster. Stacked bar + treemap drill by advertiser + brand.

entries.name: Campaign Reach & Frequency
entries.description: Reach curve (line) + frequency histogram. Plan-it "Reach vs Estimated", "Effective Reach 3+", "Reach by TRP/GRP".

entries.name: Target Discovery
entries.description: Bubble / cluster visualization of advanced target segments. Size/position encode affinity metrics.

entries.name: Network Forecaster (CPM/GRPs)
entries.description: Forecast line + dual-axis (CPM vs. GRP predictions). Trend with confidence bands.

entries.name: Brand Awareness Tracking
entries.description: Tracking line + index gauge. Brand lift over campaign. Benchmark comparison.

entries.name: Campaign ROI Index
entries.description: Scatter quadrant: ROI efficiency by channel. Surfaces highest-ROI outlets. Wendy's reference source.

entries.name: Campaign Simulator
entries.description: Interactive what-if scenario builder. Budget slider → live output projections.

entries.name: Spend Radar / Market Share
entries.description: Multi-axis radial profile. Spend vs. market opportunity comparison (currently Tableau source).

entries.name: Actual vs. Estimated (Units/TRPs)
entries.description: Comparison bars: Plan vs. Actual units delivered across networks. Variance highlighting.

entries.name: Seasonality Breakdown
entries.description: Area chart with variance bands. Q1–Q4 seasonal performance patterns. Rolling 52-week context.

entries.name: C3 Inventory & Network Yield
entries.description: Commercial ratings (C3), ad-inventory delivery, and revenue yield/min by network, daypart, and pod. Currency-grade inventory + yield tracking.

entries.name: CPM Efficiency Matrix
entries.description: CPM by network × demo × daypart vs rate card. Heatmap + rank table.

entries.name: Share of Media Report
entries.description: Expenditures and share of a brand across all 17+ media types (Network TV, Cable, Syndication, Spot TV, Radio, Magazine, Newspaper, Internet, Outdoor, etc.). Full omni-media competitive spend allocation. Distinct from AD03 (platform/network only).

entries.name: Share of Media vs. Share of Voice
entries.description: GRP delivery vs. dollar spend comparison by media type. Surfaces over- and under-delivery by channel — e.g., Cable receiving 47% of spend but delivering 78% of GRPs. Guides media mix reallocation decisions.

entries.name: Category Share of Media
entries.description: All advertiser spend within a product category across all media types. Competitive set spend landscape by category (e.g., Restaurant-Quick Svc). Shows which media types the category prioritizes and total category investment level.

entries.name: Monthly Trend — Advertiser Spending
entries.description: Month-by-month spending trend for a brand or category over a 12-month period. Surfaces seasonal spend patterns, flight timing, heavier/lighter months, and YoY budget changes. Competitive brand comparison supported.

entries.name: Period Comparison — Competitive Spend
entries.description: YoY or period-over-period spend comparison by brand/category. Answers whether a brand is increasing or decreasing their advertising budget vs. prior period. Side-by-side bar with % change column.

entries.name: Flowchart / Campaign Flight View
entries.description: Weekly GRP delivery by flight, by brand, across networks. Visual representation of a competitive media plan over a broadcast year. Shows weekly GRP values in a calendar-grid format with cumulative totals per network and brand.

entries.name: Local Market Allocation — Spot TV
entries.description: National TV dollars allocated to each DMA based on % of TV households. Compares Network/Cable/Syndicated national spend vs. Spot TV local spend by market (e.g., Chicago, LA, New York). GRP delivery + dollar comparison by market.

entries.name: A-Position / FML POD Detail View
entries.description: First/Middle/Last (FML) POD placement detail report showing total units and dollars by pod position across national TV media types. Audience retention curve by pod position. Distinct from AD01 (POD Intelligence overview) — this is the FML summary report format.

entries.name: Unit Length Trend
entries.description: Trend of commercial unit lengths (:10, :15, :30, :60) by network, category, and advertiser over time. Commercial format mix shift analysis. Shows industry migration from :30 standard to shorter or longer formats.

entries.name: Commercialization Report
entries.description: Commercial clutter by network, daypart, and category. Seconds of commercial time per hour, fill rate by pod, pod structure analysis. Shows how heavily a network commercializes its inventory vs. industry averages.

entries.name: Company / Copy Length Analysis
entries.description: Advertiser spend broken out by commercial creative length (:15 vs :30 vs :60). Creative execution efficiency lens — which length formats each advertiser or category uses most. Supports media buying and inventory pricing strategy.

entries.name: Competitive Spending Track
entries.description: Multi-year broadcast and cable network spend and total audience metrics by company. Multi-year competitive spend trajectory showing brand investment trends. Combines audience delivery with dollar spend for ROI context across competitors.

entries.name: Spending Activity Search
entries.description: Granular search for specific advertiser spend occurrences by brand, network, date, creative, and daypart. Occurrence-level drill-down into when and where a specific brand ran ads. Supports ad sales prospecting and competitive intelligence at the unit level.

entries.name: Avails Report — Linear/Broadcast
entries.description: Core rights availability: title × territory × rights × exclusivity × dates × runs remaining. Domestic, International, and Editable local-save variants.

entries.name: Monthly Available Films
entries.description: Titles available for scheduling in current month with already-utilized run counts per window.

entries.name: Films Expiring Report
entries.description: Active exhibition windows approaching expiration with unused runs — content at risk of being lost from schedule.

entries.name: Contract / Deal Status Report
entries.description: High-level deal/contract status by vendor/distributor/profit center. Active, expiring, renewed, terminated states.

entries.name: Usage Summary / Title Usage Report
entries.description: All titles aired in period for distributor — run counts, last air date, exhibition vs. free play, total programming hours.

entries.name: Usage Letter / Licensor Report (External)
entries.description: External-facing report sent to content providers — titles aired this month + historical usage vs contracted runs. Critical compliance artifact.

entries.name: Runs Remaining Report
entries.description: Contracted runs remaining per asset with cost per run. Active or expired. Exhibition Window variant available.

entries.name: Inventory Report (Asset Inventory)
entries.description: Full validated/published asset inventory — media rights, distributor, territory, network, max air date. Master content ledger.

entries.name: Media Rights Report (Territory & Rights)
entries.description: Cleared media rights by territory and asset type. Rights matrix: what is held in which markets for which content formats.

entries.name: Exhibition Restriction Report
entries.description: Restrictions against exhibition windows — conditional airing, blackout periods, authentication requirements, on-demand restrictions.

entries.name: Deal Obligations / VOD Obligations
entries.description: Contractual delivery obligations tracking — content required vs. actual deliveries each month. Marketing/promotion obligations variant.

entries.name: Royalty & Payments Report
entries.description: Deal-level royalty terms, posting history, guarantee tracking. What is owed, what has been posted, what is missing.

entries.name: Sales History Report
entries.description: Historical sales transaction ledger for Rights Out — what was sold, to whom, which territory, which period, at what fee.

entries.name: Graphical Sequential (Rights Gantt)
entries.description: Gantt-style: each title one row, color-coded bars for sold windows (by licensee) and available windows (green). Dashed = placeholder. Drag-and-drop interactive.

entries.name: Title Quick Check
entries.description: Single-title rapid avails check for live buyer calls. Available/sold status with full rights/territory/exclusivity in seconds.

entries.name: Avails Customer Package (Seller Report)
entries.description: Customer-facing formatted avails with Key Art, title metadata, 1/2/4-page templates per title. Exportable PDF/Excel/Word.

entries.name: Rights Alerts & Notifications
entries.description: System-generated alerts: acquisition expirations, output notifications, TV/theatrical premiere alerts, customer-expiring title alerts.

entries.name: Regulatory Compliance Report (Linear & VOD)
entries.description: Master lists of linear services and VOD titles, Canadian Content ratio compliance, closed captioning. CRTC-equivalent reporting.

entries.name: Deal Financial Terms Export
entries.description: Full financial deal terms — rights expressions, rate cards, revenue share calculations, guarantees, conditional financial terms.

entries.name: Deal Profitability Analysis
entries.description: Deal costs vs viewership/subscription performance. Views Per Dollar, Views Per Subscriber, modified revenue share scenarios.

entries.name: Total Video Consumption Report
entries.description: Total hours consumed across all platforms (Live+Linear, DVR, VOD, OTT/Streaming, Digital/Mobile) in single view with YoY comparison.

entries.name: Average Daily Time Spent by Device
entries.description: HH:MM daily usage by device (Live TV, Time-Shifted, Radio, ICD, App/Web Smartphone, Tablet) across age groups.

entries.name: Platform Viewing Share Report
entries.description: Share of total video consumption by platform type. Trend showing streaming growth vs. linear erosion.

entries.name: Weekly Reach by Platform & Demo
entries.description: Weekly reach % for each viewing platform by race/ethnicity with YoY delta badges. Donut badge grid visualization.

entries.name: Platform × Network KPI Summary Card
entries.description: Side-panel KPI callout with show image/logo thumbnail and single hero metric. Pull-quote companion to data tables.

entries.name: Platform Index Table
entries.description: Programs × platforms index table. Highest value per column conditionally highlighted. Performance vs. platform average index.

entries.name: Beyond L+7 Expansion Report
entries.description: L+7 audience vs 30-day complete run per program. Shows audience growth from extended OTT/VOD measurement window.

entries.name: Audience Viewing Source Venn
entries.description: Overlap of viewers across Live Only, DVR Only, OTT/VOD Only, and all combination segments for audience deduplication.

entries.name: Streaming Brand Distribution
entries.description: SVOD platform brand share of total streaming consumption. Pie/treemap by service (Netflix/Hulu/PVOD/Prime/etc).

entries.name: Streaming Share by Age Group (YoY)
entries.description: Share of total streaming minutes by age cohort (P2-11, P12-17, P18-24, P25-34, P35-54, P55+) with YoY comparison.

entries.name: Media Time by Demographic
entries.description: Daily media time (HH:MM) by race/ethnicity across all platforms. Lifestyle donut with icon center.

entries.name: Cross Platform Genre Consumption Trends
entries.description: Genre consumption bubbles over time. X=weeks, Y=total minutes viewed, bubble size=# episodes. Genre as color.

entries.name: Media Plan Gender/Demo Skew
entries.description: Gender and demographic skew index for a proposed media plan against a target audience. Diverging bar from 100-base.

entries.name: CP TRP Delivery Scorecard
entries.description: Cross-platform TRP delivery: Linear/Digital/Social/OTT. Estimate vs. Actual vs. % Diff. 2-panel paired scorecard grid.

entries.name: Video Ad Completion Rate Benchmarks
entries.description: Quarterly VCR across media type, ad length (:06/:15/:30), and device (Desktop/Mobile/Tablet/CTV). Industry benchmark.

entries.name: CTR & Time Spent Benchmarks
entries.description: Click-through rate and avg time spent with ad creative by media type and format.

entries.name: GIVT Filtered Rate Report
entries.description: % impressions filtered for invalid traffic (Spider/Bot, Data Center, Invalid Browser, High Frequency) by media type.

entries.name: Viewability & VCR Combined Report
entries.description: Viewability rate and video completion rate combined. Scatter of format efficiency by quartile completion funnel.

entries.name: OTT Ad Measurement Challenges Matrix
entries.description: 2-row table: challenge domains vs. available alternative methodologies. Qualitative OTT measurement gap framework.

entries.name: OTT Audience Profile & Engagement
entries.description: OTT audience composition by age, reach by generation, ad completion by device, hexagonal visit-length distribution.

entries.name: OTT Ad View Share by Device & Format
entries.description: OTT ad impressions by device tier (Smart TV / Mobile / Gaming). Proportional bubble with platform logos.

entries.name: Cross-Platform Reach Report (Network Ranker)
entries.description: Ranked network table with 4 column groups (Linear/Digital/Social/XPlatform Total) and Reach Curve Multi-Panel per platform.

entries.name: Social Performance Dashboard (Cross-Platform)
entries.description: Master social scorecard: followers, engagement, post volume, reach, velocity across X/Twitter, Instagram, YouTube, TikTok, Facebook — unified with period-over-period deltas.

entries.name: Follower Growth & Audience Size Report
entries.description: Absolute follower/subscriber counts + weekly growth rate (%) by platform. Benchmarks: TikTok 4.1%/mo, Instagram 1.3%, YouTube 0.8%.

entries.name: Engagement Rate by Platform & Content Type
entries.description: ER% by platform and content format (video/image/carousel/story). Benchmarks: Instagram 3.70%, TikTok 4.07%, YouTube 0.53%.

entries.name: Share of Voice Report
entries.description: Entity as % of total conversation volume within competitive set. Social equivalent of ratings share. SOV by platform over time.

entries.name: Social Buzz & Velocity Report
entries.description: Total social mentions + acceleration rate (velocity). Buzz spike area with event annotation pins (episode air, trailer drop, controversy).

entries.name: Sentiment Analysis Report
entries.description: Positive/Neutral/Negative sentiment distribution and trend over time. Topic-level sentiment by character, storyline, cast. smaSentiment field from VMN CPR.

entries.name: Social × Nielsen Ratings Correlation
entries.description: Social engagement overlaid against Nielsen linear ratings per episode date. Pearson correlation coefficient. Social lead/lag vs. ratings.

entries.name: Hashtag Performance Report
entries.description: Individual hashtag volume, reach, engagement rate, sentiment over time. Ghost Brothers (#GhostBrothers, #StraightGhosting), TUF (#TUF23) production examples.

entries.name: Influencer & Creator Performance
entries.description: Top influencers by reach, engagement, and sentiment. Ranked by userFollowers × engagements. Audience profile overlap with target demos.

entries.name: YouTube Channel & Video Performance
entries.description: Views, watch time, avg view duration, subscriber gains/losses. By device, traffic source, country. Top videos ranked. VMN CPR YouTube data structures.

entries.name: Social Content Calendar & Posting Cadence
entries.description: Post volume by day of week and time of day. Best-performing posting windows. Content format mix (video/image/text/link) vs. engagement outcome.

entries.name: Competitive Social Benchmarking
entries.description: Multi-axis radar comparing social KPIs (Followers, Engagement Rate, Posting Frequency, SOV, Growth Rate, Platform Diversity) across competitive set.

entries.name: Social × Viewership Minute Overlay
entries.description: Per-minute social PPM against per-minute Nielsen AA during live broadcast. Peak PPM moments. Commercial break social activity index.

entries.name: Platform Deep Dives (X · Instagram · YouTube · Facebook)
entries.description: Individual platform deep-dive reports for X/Twitter, Instagram, YouTube, and Facebook. Platform-native metrics, format-specific breakdowns, VMN CPR field-level data structures.

entries.name: Twitter/X Monthly Analytics Dashboard
entries.description: KPI strip + delta badges (Followers/Impressions/Interactions → Replies/Retweets/Favorites), engagement by action type, Top 10 posts by interaction rate. RSG production template (Sept 2014).
tags: Delta Badge Row (CT-NEW-14) + Horiz. Bar + Donut (CT-NEW-13)

entries.name: Twitter Sentiment & Audience Profile
entries.description: Sentiment breakdown, geo heat map (smaAuthorCountry/State), gender split, audience interests, consumer buying style, influencer identification. Big Knowledge Twitter App.

entries.name: Twitter Hashtag Tracking (Live Show)
entries.description: Real-time + historical tracking of specific hashtags during a live airing. Ghost Brothers (#GhostBrothers, #StraightGhosting) and TUF (#TUF23, #TUF) production examples.

entries.name: Facebook Page Insights Dashboard
entries.description: Page fans, impressions, video views (organic/paid/10s/30s/complete), emoji reactions. Multi-network + single-network/series drill-down. VMN CPR FB_Page_Metrics data structure.

entries.name: YouTube Channel & Video Analytics Dashboard
entries.description: Views, Watch_Time_Minutes, Average_View_Duration, Annotation CTR, Card CTR, Subscribers_Gained/Lost. By Country, Device Type (100-105), Traffic Source (0-18). Exact VMN CPR YouTube data structures.

entries.name: Cross-Platform Social Integration Column
entries.description: Social Mentions (000) and Facebook Engagement Rate embedded inline within the linear ratings table alongside Nielsen L+SD/L+3/L+7/C3. Comedy Central Idiotsitter Dashboard V12 production precedent.

entries.name: Site Traffic / Site Overview Report
entries.description: Unique Visitors, Visits, Page Views, Time Spent, Bounce Rate, New vs. Returning over time.

entries.name: Ranked Report (Top N)
entries.description: Top N items for any metric sorted by value. Pages, Site Sections, Products, Videos by visits/views/revenue.

entries.name: Trended Report (Metric Over Time)
entries.description: Any metric tracked across time periods for up to 5 items simultaneously. Trend Line, Smooth Line (video), Area, Stacked Area variants.

entries.name: Conversion Funnel Report
entries.description: % of visitors progressing through defined event sequence (Visit → Cart → Checkout → Purchase). Fallout rate at each step.

entries.name: Fallout Report (Path Analysis)
entries.description: Visitors through pre-specified page sequence. Conversion AND fallout rates between each checkpoint. Non-linear branching paths supported.

entries.name: Page Flow / Next Page Report
entries.description: Tree-based visualization of visitor page order from a selected page. Line widths proportional to traffic share. 3 levels deep. Forward or backward variants.

entries.name: Marketing Channel Report
entries.description: Which external channels drive traffic and conversion. First Touch vs. Last Touch attribution. Paid/Organic/Social/Display/Email/Direct split.

entries.name: Visitor Retention Report
entries.description: Weekly cohort-based visitor return rate. Day 1/7/14/30 retention curves. New vs. returning visitor composition over time.

entries.name: Visitor Profile (Geographic)
entries.description: Visitor country/state/city distribution. DMA choropleth for US-focused sites. Language + locale breakdown. Mobile vs. desktop by geography.

entries.name: Video Engagement / Smooth Line Report
entries.description: % of views for each video time segment. Dropout curve. Rises indicate replay/rewind. Peaks identify most-rewatched moments.

entries.name: Products / Conversion Report
entries.description: Product-level revenue, units, conversion rate, average order value. Category hierarchy with drill-down. Subscription vs. transactional splits.

entries.name: Custom Traffic Report (Prop)
entries.description: Custom traffic reporting using Omniture/Adobe Traffic Properties (Props). Counter, list, and hierarchical prop types. Pathing enabled.

entries.name: Custom Conversion Report (eVar)
entries.description: Custom conversion reporting using eVar variables. Linear, participation, and last-touch allocation. Campaign-level conversion attribution.

entries.name: Segmented / Breakdown Report
entries.description: Any report broken down by a second dimension. Traffic × Traffic (correlations) or Conversion × Conversion (subrelations). Up to 20 levels deep. 3-Metric Bubble variant.

entries.name: Media Universe Map Report
entries.description: Market-cap-scaled solar system of ~125 entities (Big Tech, legacy media, gaming, audio, social, video) redrawn monthly from public data. 2025 restructure adds Creator Economy, AI, Measurement, Global platforms, PSM. Feb 2026: first version where Alphabet > Apple.

entries.name: Quarterly Earnings Intelligence Report
entries.description: Earnings call transcript vs actual financial numbers delta for 100+ publicly traded media companies per quarter. Covers Versant, Canal+, ITV, Netflix, Disney, WBD, Paramount, Fox. Tracks trajectory shifts before they appear in market prices.

entries.name: Attention Share Report (Cross-Screen Index)
entries.description: The ESHAP Cross-Screen Attention Index — first measurement of the whole viewer across all screens simultaneously. Published 'against great resistance from Delta Airlines.' Minutes of attention per entity, per platform, per demographic cohort.

entries.name: Streaming Platform Landscape Analysis
entries.description: SVOD/AVOD/FAST landscape: subscriber counts, viewership share, revenue trajectory, content spend, ad-supported tier penetration, bundling, and consolidation. ESHAP YouTube Landscape Analysis (Summer 2025) as standalone sub-report.

entries.name: YouTube on TV Intelligence Report
entries.description: YouTube CTV viewership vs. all traditional TV entities — monthly rank tracking since April 2025 (#1 US TV). Beats all Disney channels combined, all NBCU, Paramount+CBS, Fox+Fox News. Excludes YouTube TV, mobile, desktop, Shorts.

entries.name: Creator Economy Market Report
entries.description: Creator economy scale vs. legacy media: revenue, audience, valuation, growth. 'Yindie Film' case studies. 2026 'Worlds Collide' thesis: Trad Media + Creators unite. Validates 'half the map' projection: Netflix now worth more than Comcast + Disney + Paramount + Fox + WBD combined.

entries.name: Media's Generation Gap Report
entries.description: ESHAP standalone report (Summer 2025): viewing habits, platform preferences, attention duration, and content format affinity by Gen Z / Millennial / Gen X / Boomer. Validates 'younger consumers pay more than any other cohort when content earns their affinity.'

entries.name: Cord-Cutter / Streaming Migration Tracker
entries.description: Pay TV household erosion model vs. streaming net subscriber adds. AVOD tier growth. 'Winner-take-most' consolidation trajectory. SVOD price hike impact on churn vs. ad-tier adoption. Amazon as 'new cable company' (Shapiro 2025 thesis).
